what_it_does: Renders a generic, schema-driven configuration form for any node type. It reads the node type's config_schema from the live registry, converts it into labeled fields, renders each as either a <select> or an <input>, coerces edited values, and calls an onSet callback to commit changes.
observations: Fields are derived entirely from config_schema via configSchemaToFields.
Select fields are populated from f.options; text/number fields use uncontrolled defaultValue with onBlur commit.
A current select value that is missing from the live options is still surfaced as an explicit '(current)' option.
Number inputs use type='number' based on f.rawType; everything else uses type='text'.
Pressing Enter in an input triggers blur to commit.
The component is wrapped by PanelErrorBoundary at the call site, per the file comment.
The comment states the goal is zero per-type code for new node types.
standouts: Fail-loud rendering of a current select value that is absent from live options, avoiding silent drops.
Fully generic form generation from registry schema with no per-node-type branching.
conventions: React functional component with inline prop type annotation.
data-ui-ref attribute used for UI reference/testing.
Comment header marks origin (App.tsx:320-355) and PRESERVE-LIST item.
concerns: Text inputs use defaultValue and onBlur rather than a controlled value, so parent-driven config updates may not refresh the input.
Select values are coerced to String, which may mangle non-string primitives or objects.
No visible validation or error handling beyond coerceConfigValue.
No debouncing; every blur/change immediately calls onSet.
notes: A generic, registry-schema-driven node inspector form that renders selects and inputs and commits edits through a simple callback.
questions: What is the exact shape returned by getOINFO and configSchemaToFields?
How does options_from populate f.options for model dropdowns?
What types and edge cases does coerceConfigValue handle?
Why are text inputs uncontrolled (defaultValue) while the select is controlled?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders a generic form from config_schema and commits edits via onSet.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth from registry metadata
intention_signals: The file is trying to make node configuration fully data-driven so that adding a new node type requires no new inspector form code, while ensuring stale or unregistered current values remain visible rather than being silently discarded.
novelty: The explicit '(current)' fallback option for select values missing from the live option list, combined with the stated zero-per-node-type-code design goal.
contribution: Provides a reusable, schema-driven configuration form that renders arbitrary node-type fields from registry metadata, commits changes through a simple per-field callback, and preserves out-of-list current values for transparency.
summary_for_embedding: NodeConfigForm is a generic React component that renders a node type's config_schema as select and input fields, coerces edited values, and propagates them via an onSet callback.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to eliminate per-node-type inspector form code by deriving fields entirely from the registry's live config_schema.

symbols.code_id: code://company/canvas/app/src/components/NodeConfigForm.tsx::NodeConfigForm | component | export function NodeConfigForm({ nodeType, config, onSet }:
symbols.does: Renders a generic configuration form for a node type based on its registry config_schema and propagates edits through onSet.
symbols.description: NodeConfigForm is a React functional component that takes a nodeType, a config object, and an onSet callback. It retrieves the node type's config_schema from the registry store, converts it into an array of fields, and renders each as a controlled <select> or an uncontrolled <input>. Edits are coerced and committed via onSet on blur or select change, with special handling to surface current select values that are no longer present in the live option list.
